questions.q: In the Puranas, the prince Dhruva is best known for becoming which celestial body?
questions.explanation: According to the Bhagavata Purana, Dhruva was fixed in the heavens as the immovable Pole Star (Dhruva-tara).

questions.q: In the story of Dhruva, which deity did the young prince undertake intense penance to seek?
questions.explanation: Dhruva performed severe austerities in the forest to obtain the vision of Vishnu, who appeared and blessed him.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, what was the social rank of Dhruva by birth?
questions.explanation: Dhruva was a young prince, the son of King Uttanapada.

questions.q: In the tale of Dhruva, who slighted the boy and prompted him to leave for the forest?
questions.explanation: Dhruva was insulted by his stepmother Suruchi, which set him on the path of penance.

questions.q: In the Puranas, who is named as the royal father of Dhruva?
questions.explanation: Dhruva was the son of King Uttanapada, an early monarch in the Puranic genealogies.

questions.q: What quality does the story of Dhruva, the Pole Star, most famously symbolize in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Dhruva is honored as an emblem of unwavering, steadfast devotion to the divine.

questions.q: In which natural setting did the boy Dhruva carry out his great penance?
questions.explanation: Dhruva left the palace and went into the forest to perform his austerities seeking Vishnu.

questions.q: As the Pole Star, what is the special property of Dhruva among all the stars in the sky?
options: It remains fixed while others revolve around it
questions.explanation: Dhruva is described as the immovable star around which all other stars appear to revolve.

questions.q: In the story, what state did Vishnu grant to the devoted boy Dhruva?
options: A thousand-year life as a king
questions.explanation: Vishnu, pleased by his devotion, granted Dhruva an exalted and unshakable position in the heavens.

questions.q: The narrative of Dhruva is most fully told in which body of scripture?
questions.explanation: The detailed account of Dhruva appears in the Puranas, especially the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, what was the name of Dhruva's mother, the first queen of Uttanapada?
questions.explanation: Dhruva's mother was Suniti, the elder and devout queen of King Uttanapada.

questions.q: In the Puranic account, what was the name of the favored stepmother who insulted Dhruva?
questions.explanation: Suruchi, the favored queen, scornfully told Dhruva he could not sit on his father's lap.

questions.q: In the story of Dhruva, what privilege did the stepmother Suruchi deny him, sparking his quest?
options: Sitting on the king's lap or throne
A share of the royal treasury
questions.explanation: Suruchi told the boy he had no right to sit on the king's lap or aspire to the throne, wounding him deeply.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, which celestial sage guided and instructed Dhruva on his way to the forest?
questions.explanation: The sage Narada met the determined boy, tested his resolve, and taught him how to worship Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Puranas, on the banks of which sacred forest river did Dhruva perform his austerities?
questions.explanation: Narada directed Dhruva to the forest of Madhuvana on the banks of the Yamuna for his penance.

questions.q: According to tradition, which sacred mantra did the sage Narada teach Dhruva to repeat during his penance?
questions.explanation: Narada instructed Dhruva in the twelve-syllable mantra 'Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya' to invoke Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, as Dhruva's penance intensified, what extraordinary discipline did he maintain regarding his breath?
options: He stood on one leg and restrained his breathing
He chanted only while submerged in water
questions.explanation: Dhruva is described standing on one leg in deep meditation, regulating his breath until the worlds trembled.

questions.q: In the story of Dhruva, in which divine form did Vishnu finally appear to the boy?
options: As a brilliant light with no form
As a four-armed figure holding conch and discus
questions.explanation: Vishnu appeared before Dhruva in his characteristic four-armed form bearing the conch, discus, mace and lotus.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, with what object did Vishnu touch Dhruva to inspire his hymn of praise?
questions.explanation: Vishnu touched Dhruva's forehead with his conch, and divine knowledge welled up so the boy could praise the Lord.

questions.q: In the Puranic narrative, how old was Dhruva, roughly, when he undertook his celebrated penance?
options: A grown king of many years
A small child of about five years
questions.explanation: Dhruva is portrayed as a very young child, around five years old, when he set out to win Vishnu's grace.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, what did Dhruva do after attaining Vishnu's vision and before ascending to his celestial seat?
options: He returned home and ruled as a righteous king
He renounced the world at once and vanished
He drowned himself in the Yamuna
questions.explanation: Dhruva returned to his father, was welcomed, and ruled the kingdom righteously for many years before ascending.

questions.q: Which broad family of Hindu devotion is Dhruva most associated with, given the deity he worshipped?
questions.explanation: Because Dhruva worshipped Vishnu and won his grace, his story is cherished within the Vaishnava tradition.

questions.q: In the Puranic genealogy, King Uttanapada, the father of Dhruva, is said to be the son of which primordial figure?
questions.explanation: Uttanapada is named as a son of Svayambhuva Manu, placing Dhruva early in the Puranic lineage of humankind.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, what name did Vishnu give to the supreme, abode-like station granted to Dhruva?
questions.explanation: Vishnu bestowed upon Dhruva the exalted, eternal station known as Dhruvaloka or Dhruvapada, the Pole Star seat.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, what is the name of the celestial vehicle (vimana) sent to carry Dhruva to his heavenly abode?
options: A vimana sent by Vishnu's attendants Sunanda and Nanda
questions.explanation: Vishnu's two attendants, Sunanda and Nanda, descended in a shining vimana to bear Dhruva upward.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, an encounter with whom prompted Dhruva to wage a fierce war during his reign?
options: The Yakshas, after one slew his brother Uttama
questions.explanation: After a Yaksha killed his half-brother Uttama, Dhruva fought a great war against the Yakshas until Manu calmed him.

questions.q: In the Puranas, who intervened to halt Dhruva's terrible war against the Yakshas and counsel him toward peace?
questions.explanation: Svayambhuva Manu appeared and persuaded Dhruva to cease the slaughter, after which Dhruva was reconciled with the Yakshas' lord Kubera.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, which lord of wealth, pleased with Dhruva's restraint, offered him a boon after the Yaksha war?
questions.explanation: Kubera, lord of the Yakshas and of wealth, was pleased and granted Dhruva the boon of unbroken remembrance of Vishnu.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the great sages depicted as the Saptarishi (Big Dipper) are said to do what in relation to Dhruva?
options: Circle eternally around Dhruva, the fixed Pole Star
Carry Dhruva across the sky each night
questions.explanation: The seven sages of the Saptarishi constellation are described as perpetually revolving around the steadfast Dhruva.

questions.q: Besides the Bhagavata Purana, in which other major Purana is the story of Dhruva prominently narrated?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu Purana also recounts the tale of Dhruva at length, alongside the better-known Bhagavata Purana version.
